title: Earnings Call Transcript: Peloton Misses EPS but Beats Revenue in Q3 2026
text: Peloton Q3 2026 Earnings Call Transcript Analysis. Earnings Results Summary: Peloton reported Q3 revenue of $631 million, beating expectations of $618.74 million. However, earnings per share of $0.06 missed the forecast of $0.07. Revenue Beat Details: The company exceeded its own guidance range of $605-625M, indicating better-than-expected demand for connected fitness equipment. EPS Miss Analysis: Despite the revenue beat, profitability metrics fell short due to margin pressures and operational costs. Strategic Commentary: Management highlighted the importance of the Spotify partnership and commercial expansion as key drivers of future growth. Subscriber Update: Connected fitness subscriptions declined 7.6% year-over-year to 2.662 million, but churn improved to 1.2%, showing stabilization. Capital Allocation: With $173 million in net debt (down 70% YoY), management discussed strategic optionality including potential debt refinancing, share repurchases, and targeted investments. Forward Guidance: Full-year revenue guidance of $2.42-2.44 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $470-480 million reflects management's confidence in achieving full-year profitability.
